reference_answer: In Object Oriented Programming, there are two main types of constructors: default constructors and parameterized constructors. Default constructors do not take any parameters and are used to initialize objects with default values, while parameterized constructors take one or more parameters and allow for customized initialization of objects. This is in contrast to destructors, which are special member functions that are called when an object goes out of scope and are used to release resources held by the object.
